primary_meaning: to make something clear or easy to understand
usage_notes: Follows pattern: explain something to someone, explain that...
common_collocations: explain something, explain how, explain why, explain the reason
image_search_query: teacher explaining something to student on whiteboard
image_context: A teacher pointing at a whiteboard while a student listens attentively with a confused then understanding expression
example_1: Can you explain how this machine works?
example_1_translation: Você pode explicar como esta máquina funciona?
example_2: She explained the problem to the team.
example_2_translation: Ela explicou o problema para a equipe.
example_3: He couldn't explain why he was late.
example_3_translation: Ele não conseguiu explicar por que estava atrasado.
pronunciation_tips: Emphasize second syllable: ex-PLAIN. The 'ex' sounds like 'igs'
memory_hook: Ex-plain - lay out the meaning completely
common_mistakes: Not 'explain me' - say 'explain to me'

primary_meaning: to say what someone or something is like
secondary_meaning: to give details about an event
usage_notes: Follows pattern: describe something to someone
common_collocations: describe something, describe how, describe in detail
image_search_query: person describing a scene to another person
image_context: A person gesturing and talking while another person listens carefully, perhaps looking at a photograph
example_2: She described what happened that night.
example_2_translation: Ela descreveu o que aconteceu naquela noite.
example_3: The police asked him to describe the car.
example_3_translation: A polícia pediu para ele descrever o carro.
pronunciation_tips: Emphasize second syllable: de-SCRIBE. The 'sc' sounds like 'sk'
memory_hook: De-scribe - write down the details (from Latin scribere)
common_mistakes: Spell with 'sc' not 'sk'

primary_meaning: to put forward an idea or plan for someone to consider
secondary_meaning: to indicate or hint at something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: suggest something, suggest doing something, suggest that...
common_collocations: suggest something, suggest doing, suggest an idea
image_search_query: person suggesting an idea in a meeting with hand gesture
image_context: A person in a meeting pointing to a document or making a hand gesture while others consider the idea
example_1: Can you suggest a good restaurant?
example_1_translation: Você pode sugerir um bom restaurante?
example_2: I suggest we meet at six o'clock.
example_2_translation: Eu sugiro que nos encontremos às seis.
example_3: Her expression suggested she was angry.
example_3_translation: A expressão dela sugeriu que ela estava brava.
pronunciation_tips: The 'su' sounds like 'suh', 'gg' sounds like 'j'. Emphasize second syllable: sug-GEST
memory_hook: Sug-gest - throw an idea out for consideration
common_mistakes: Not 'suggest to do' - say 'suggest doing'

primary_meaning: to present something for someone to accept or reject
secondary_meaning: to say you will do something for someone
usage_notes: Follows pattern: offer something, offer to do, offer someone something
common_collocations: offer help, offer something, offer to help, accept an offer
image_search_query: person offering a cup of coffee to another person
image_context: A host offering a cup of coffee with a welcoming smile, the other person considering the offer
example_1: Can I offer you a cup of tea?
example_1_translation: Posso oferecer uma xícara de chá?
example_2: He offered to drive us to the station.
example_2_translation: Ele ofereceu para nos levar à estação.
example_3: They offered me a new job.
example_3_translation: Eles me ofereceram um novo emprego.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: OF-fer. 'Of' like 'off', 'fer' like 'fur' without the r sound
memory_hook: Of-fer - bring something forward
common_mistakes: Not 'offer for you' - say 'offer to you' or just 'offer'

primary_meaning: to say no to something or someone
usage_notes: Follows pattern: refuse something, refuse to do
common_collocations: refuse to do, refuse help, refuse permission
image_search_query: person shaking head no with hand gesture
image_context: A person with arms crossed or hand raised in a stopping gesture, shaking their head firmly
example_1: She refused to answer the question.
example_1_translation: Ela se recusou a responder a pergunta.
example_3: They refused him permission to leave.
example_3_translation: Eles recusaram a permissão dele para sair.
pronunciation_tips: Emphasize second syllable: re-FUSE. The 're' sounds like 'ri'
memory_hook: Re-fuse - put back the fuse, don't let it flow
common_mistakes: Not 'refuse for doing' - say 'refuse to do'

primary_meaning: to say yes to something that has been offered
usage_notes: Follows pattern: accept something, accept that...
common_collocations: accept an offer, accept responsibility, accept the fact
image_search_query: person happily accepting a gift or document
image_context: A person with a happy smile reaching out to take a gift box or document being handed to them
example_2_translation: Ela aceitou a oferta de emprego.
example_3: He can't accept that he's wrong.
example_3_translation: Ele não consegue aceitar que está errado.
pronunciation_tips: Emphasize second syllable: ac-CEPT. The 'ac' is weak
memory_hook: Ac-cept - draw something toward you
common_mistakes: Pronounce the 'p' clearly, not 'acsept'

primary_meaning: to know the meaning or importance of something
usage_notes: Irregular past: understood. Often used with 'can' for ability
common_collocations: understand something, understand how, make understood
image_search_query: person having a moment of understanding with lightbulb
image_context: A person with an 'aha' expression, perhaps with a lightbulb appearing above their head
example_1: I don't understand what you mean.
example_1_translation: Eu não entendo o que você quer dizer.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: un-der-STAND. Emphasize third: un-der-STAND
memory_hook: Under-stand - stand under the meaning
common_mistakes: Past tense is 'understood', not 'understanded'

primary_meaning: to become aware of something you did not notice before
usage_notes: British spelling: realise. American: realize
common_collocations: realize something, realize that, suddenly realize
image_search_query: person having sudden realization with expression change
image_context: A person's face changing from confusion to understanding, hand on forehead in an 'aha' moment
example_1: I realized I was in the wrong place.
example_1_translation: Eu percebi que estava no lugar errado.
example_2: She didn't realize how late it was.
example_2_translation: Ela não percebeu como era tarde.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: RE-al-ize. Emphasize first: RE-a-lize
memory_hook: Re-al-ize - make it real in your mind
common_mistakes: Not 'realize about' - say 'realize that' or just 'realize something'

primary_meaning: to find something new or for the first time
secondary_meaning: to learn something you did not know
usage_notes: Follows pattern: discover something, discover that...
common_collocations: discover something, discover a secret, discover the truth
image_search_query: explorer discovering a new land or treasure
image_context: An explorer finding something exciting like a hidden cave entrance or ancient artifact with wonder on their face
example_3: I discovered she was a doctor.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: dis-CO-ver. Emphasize second: dis-CO-ver
memory_hook: Dis-cover - remove the cover, reveal what was hidden
common_mistakes: Not 'discover by accident' - it's already about finding

primary_meaning: to come into view or existence
usage_notes: Often used with 'to be' or 'like'. Past tense: appeared
common_collocations: appear suddenly, appear tired, appear to be
image_search_query: magician making something appear from thin air
image_context: A person looking surprised as something materializes or comes into view unexpectedly
example_1: A man appeared at the door.
example_2: She appears to be very happy.
example_3: The plane appeared on the radar.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: ap-PEAR. Emphasize second: ap-PEAR
memory_hook: Ap-pear - to come into view
common_mistakes: Not 'appear good' - say 'appear to be good' or 'look good'

primary_meaning: to show that something is true
secondary_meaning: to test something to see if it works
usage_notes: Past tense: proved. Often used in passive: be proved
common_collocations: prove something, prove that, prove to be
image_search_query: detective proving a point with evidence
image_context: A detective pointing at evidence on a table, showing proof to solve a case
example_3: She proved she was a good leader.
example_3_translation: Ela provou que era uma boa líder.
pronunciation_tips: One syllable: PROOV. No 'r' sound after 'v'
memory_hook: Prove - show it to be true
common_mistakes: Pronounce carefully - no 'r' sound at the end

primary_meaning: to make something better
usage_notes: Can be used as verb or intransitive: improve your skills, skills improve
common_collocations: improve something, improve health, improve skills
image_search_query: graph showing improvement going upward
image_context: A business person watching a positive growth chart going upward, expressing satisfaction
example_1: I need to improve my English.
example_3: We need to improve the service.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: im-PROVE. Emphasize second: im-PROVE
memory_hook: Im-prove - make it more pro
common_mistakes: Not 'improve more better' - just 'improve'

primary_meaning: to be in charge of something or someone
secondary_meaning: to succeed in doing something difficult
usage_notes: Follows pattern: manage something, manage to do
common_collocations: manage a team, manage to do, manage time
image_search_query: manager at work organizing and directing team
image_context: A manager looking at multiple tasks and resources, organizing and directing with confidence
example_1: She manages a team of ten people.
example_1_translation: Ela gerencia uma equipe de dez pessoas.
example_2: I managed to finish on time.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: MAN-age. Emphasize first: MAN-age
memory_hook: Man-age - handle with skill
common_mistakes: Not 'manage for doing' - say 'manage to do'

primary_meaning: to include or contain something
secondary_meaning: to make someone participate in something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: involve something, involve someone in
common_collocations: involve something, involve someone in, be involved in
image_search_query: people working together on a project
image_context: Several people around a table working on a project together, representing participation and inclusion
example_1: The project involves a lot of work.
example_2: She was involved in the accident.
example_3: Don't involve me in your problems.
example_3_translation: Não me envolva nos seus problemas.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: in-VOLVE. Emphasize second: in-VOLVE
memory_hook: In-volve - roll something into something else
common_mistakes: Not 'involve with' - say 'involve in'

primary_meaning: to contain as part of something
secondary_meaning: to make someone part of something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: include something, include someone in
common_collocations: include something, include someone, be included
image_search_query: adding ingredients to a recipe list
image_context: Someone adding items to a list or recipe, each ingredient being included in the whole
example_1_translation: O preço inclui café da manhã.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: in-CLUDE. Emphasize second: in-CLUDE
memory_hook: In-clude - close things in
common_mistakes: Not 'include to do' - say 'include doing' or 'include something'

primary_meaning: to need something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: require something, require to do
common_collocations: require something, require attention, require patience
image_search_query: job application with requirements listed
image_context: A document listing job requirements or a person needing certain tools to complete a task
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: re-QUIRE. Emphasize second: re-QUIRE
memory_hook: Re-quire - require again, demand back
common_mistakes: Not 'require for doing' - say 'require to do' or 'require something'

primary_meaning: to show or point to something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: indicate something, indicate that...
common_collocations: indicate something, indicate the direction, indicate that
image_search_query: road sign indicating direction
image_context: A road sign pointing in a direction or a person pointing at something to show where it is
example_2: Her answer indicated she was happy.
example_2_translation: A resposta dela indicou que ela estava feliz.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: IN-di-cate. Emphasize first: IN-di-cate
memory_hook: In-dicate - point something out
common_mistakes: Not 'indicate at' - say 'indicate something' or 'indicate that'

primary_meaning: to be a symbol or sign of something
secondary_meaning: to act on behalf of someone
usage_notes: Follows pattern: represent something, represent someone
common_collocations: represent something, represent the country, represent a company
image_search_query: flag representing a country
image_context: A country's flag waving or displayed, representing the nation and its people
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: rep-re-SENT. Emphasize last: rep-re-SENT
memory_hook: Rep-re-sent - present again, stand in someone's place
common_mistakes: Pronounce the 're' clearly, not 'pre-sent'

primary_meaning: to be owned by someone
secondary_meaning: to be a member of a group
usage_notes: Follows pattern: belong to someone/something
common_collocations: belong to someone, belong to a group, belong here
image_search_query: item with name label showing ownership
image_context: Personal items with name labels or people in group settings showing membership and belonging
example_2: I belong to a sports club.
example_2_translation: Eu pertenço a um clube esportivo.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: be-LONG. Emphasize second: be-LONG
memory_hook: Be-long - be along with, be part of
common_mistakes: Always use 'to' - belong to, not belong with or belong for

primary_meaning: to need someone or something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: depend on/upon someone/something
common_collocations: depend on, depend upon, it depends
image_search_query: person relying on another for support
image_context: One person leaning on another for support, showing trust and reliance in a relationship
example_1: I depend on my family for support.
example_1_translation: Eu dependo da minha família para apoio.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: de-PEND. Emphasize second: de-PEND
memory_hook: De-pend - hang from, rely on
common_mistakes: Always 'depend on' or 'depend upon', never just 'depend' alone for this meaning

primary_meaning: to say yes to something
usage_notes: Follows pattern: agree to something, agree with someone, agree that...
common_collocations: agree with, agree to, agree on, agree that
image_search_query: two people nodding and shaking hands in agreement
image_context: Two business people shaking hands or nodding together with satisfied expressions, reaching an agreement
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: a-GREE. Emphasize second: a-GREE
memory_hook: A-gree - be in harmony, say yes
common_mistakes: Not 'agree with that' for facts - say 'I agree that...' or 'That's true'

primary_meaning: to make someone feel unexpected emotion
usage_notes: Follows pattern: surprise someone, surprise someone with something
common_collocations: surprise someone, surprise party, be surprised
image_search_query: birthday surprise party with person shocked
image_context: A person with a surprised expression at a birthday party, seeing unexpected guests or gifts
example_2: I surprised her with a gift.
example_2_translation: Eu a surpreendi com um presente.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: sur-PRISE. Emphasize second: sur-PRISE
memory_hook: Sur-prise - rise over, come upon suddenly
common_mistakes: Not 'very surprise' - say 'very surprised' or 'a big surprise'

primary_meaning: to make someone angry or impatient
usage_notes: Often used with 'be annoyed with' or 'at'
common_collocations: annoy someone, be annoyed with, annoy at
image_search_query: person looking annoyed with hands on hips
image_context: A person with an annoyed expression, perhaps arms crossed or hands on hips, showing frustration
example_3_translation: O comportamento dele realmente a irritou.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: an-NOY. Emphasize second: an-NOY
memory_hook: An-noy - make noise against, disturb
common_mistakes: Not 'annoy with' someone - say 'annoyed with' or 'annoyed at'
